author: Leandro Voltolino
description: A floating, Capacities-style table of contents with a minimap on the edge, active-heading tracking, click-to-scroll and document stats. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Leandro Voltolino
manifest.description: A floating, Capacities-style table of contents: an edge minimap for headings with active-heading tracking and click-to-scroll, plus an open-tasks tab with a live count and optional one-click completion.
manifest.name: Subtle TOC

name: Subtle TOC

releases.author: xupisco
releases.description:
## v0.4.0 — Readable rows, and a TOC that behaves

Long tasks and headings are no longer cut off, and the popover stopped closing
when you didn't ask it to. Plus a handful of knobs to make it feel like yours.

### ✨ New

- **Multi-line rows** *(on by default)* — long headings and tasks now wrap and
  show in full instead of being cut with an ellipsis. Prefer the compact look?
  Turn **Show multiple lines** off and every row goes back to a single line, with
  the full text in a tooltip on hover.
- **Default tab** — pick whether **Headings** or **Tasks** leads the tab bar and
  opens first. Your last-used tab is still remembered after that, and it always
  falls back to the tab that actually has content.
- **Close delay** — a slider (0–1000 ms) for how long the popover waits before
  closing once the mouse leaves it. Raise it if it closed on you while switching
  to a shorter tab.
- **Leave sideways to dismiss** — moving the mouse out of the popover back toward
  the note closes it immediately, delay or not. Exits up, down, or back to the
  edge still get the grace period.
- **Active tab color** — set the selected tab's background, with a reset button to
  hand it back to your theme.
- **Show tasks in minimap** — hide the open-task badge on the edge. Notes with
  tasks but no headings always keep it, so the TOC stays reachable.

### ⚙️ Notes

- **Show multiple lines** defaults to on, so rows that used to be cut on one line
  will now wrap. Toggle it off in settings for the previous look.
- No breaking changes; every existing setting keeps its behavior and value.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/xupisco/obisidian-suble-toc/compare/0.3.0...0.4.0

releases.author: xupisco
releases.description:
## v0.3.0 — Tasks in the TOC

The Subtle TOC now surfaces the active note's **open tasks** alongside your headings,
while the edge minimap stays focused on structure.

### ✨ New

- **Tasks tab** in the popover — the note's open tasks (unchecked checkboxes) as a
  flat list, in document order. Click a task to jump to it (smooth scroll + line
  flash, in Editing and Reading mode), just like headings.
- **Open-task count** shown as a chip on the Tasks tab.
- **Edge task badge** — a checkbox + count appears on the edge whenever the note has
  open tasks: below the dashes when there are headings, or on its own when there
  aren't.
- **Complete a task from the TOC** *(opt-in)* — enable **Show task checkboxes** to
  tick a task straight from the popover. It's marked done in the note (undoable in
  Editing mode), strikes through, and drops off the list the next time you open it.
- **Show** setting to choose what to surface: **Headings**, **Tasks**, or **Both**.

### ⚙️ Behavior

- Default tab is picked by the note's content, and your last-used tab is preserved
  across opens.
- Everything is always scoped to the active note — switch notes and the TOC rebuilds.
- The heading-level range setting doesn't apply to tasks.
- Tasks come from Obsidian's `metadataCache`, so the list stays in sync as you type
  (and updates when a task is checked/unchecked in the note).

### 🔧 Settings

- **Show** — Headings / Tasks / Both.
- **Show task checkboxes** — off by default; turn on to complete tasks from the TOC.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/xupisco/obisidian-suble-toc/compare/0.2.1...0.3.0
